Container.Name: FanBridge
Container.Overview:
FanBridge 1.4.0 reads Unraid disk temperatures, calculates fan demand, and can control one or more external FanBridge Link PWM controllers over USB serial when automatic output is enabled.
Run the container unprivileged. The host /dev directory is visible read-only at /host-dev so serial nodes track hotplug events, while Docker cgroup rules permit only USB ACM controllers and RP2040 BOOTSEL character devices. Select the stable /host-dev/serial/by-id path when adding each controller.
Map /var/local/emhttp to /unraid read-only and leave the 600-second stale threshold in place when Unraid poll_attributes is set to the recommended 300 seconds. To upload DIY RP2040 UF2 firmware from the UI, retain the /dev/bus/usb mapping and USB character-device rule; FanBridge remains non-privileged and accepts only validated RP2040 UF2 images. On first run, use the setup token written to /config/setup.token and shown once in the container log to create the administrator account.
Container.Description: Unraid disk-temperature monitoring and external PWM fan-controller bridge.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the FanBridge web interface.

Config.Name: AppData
Config.Description: Persistent configuration, users, session secret, setup token, and history.

Config.Name: Unraid emhttp directory
Config.Description: Read-only directory containing the live disks.ini file. Map the directory, not an overlapping single-file bind.

Config.Name: Hotplug serial devices
Config.Description: Read-only device-directory view used to discover /host-dev/serial/by-id controllers after unplug, reconnect, or firmware reboot. Device cgroup rules still restrict access to USB ACM and BOOTSEL character devices.

Config.Name: RP2040 firmware USB bus
Config.Description: Required only for in-app DIY RP2040 UF2 flashing. Provides dynamic BOOTSEL USB nodes without privileged mode; remove this mapping to disable firmware uploads.

Config.Name: Runtime home
Config.Description: Writable runtime home for the read-only container.

Config.Name: Maintenance mode
Config.Description: Set to 1 only while using manual PWM or fan-test controls. Read-only serial diagnostics and verified RP2040 firmware uploads do not require it.

Config.Name: First-run setup token
Config.Description: Optional. Leave blank to generate /config/setup.token and print it once at startup. If preset, use a long random value and treat the saved template as sensitive.

Config.Name: Stale temperature threshold (seconds)
Config.Description: Treat disks.ini as stale after this interval. The recommended 600 seconds allows one missed update when Unraid poll_attributes is 300 seconds.

Config.Name: Secure cookies
Config.Description: Set to 1 only when the Web UI is served through HTTPS; browsers will then send the session cookie over HTTPS only.
